Job Description: About the Role: The analytics team’s mission is to empower Cityblock with trustworthy data insights, intuitive and reliable tools, and deep data domain expertise in support of our mission to radically improve the health of marginalized communities at scale. The Senior Data Analyst in this role should have a strong sense of curiosity, a propensity towards action, and a desire to work in agile teams. In this role, you should feel comfortable working with and advising experienced business executives, technical stakeholders, engineers, product managers, and clinical teams. This analyst will act as a technical mentor and leader across the analytics team to assist with code reviews, question design, and stakeholder management. In this position, you will support leaders to drive clinical and operational analytics and design data models with data engineers. Additionally, you will build new data-based tools to solve complex operational problems, leveraging Generative AI, Large Language Models (LLMs), and conversational analytics to automate insights and enhance natural language data querying capabilities. Responsibilities: Partner with senior leaders across operations, product, and clinical teams to transform data into strategy and business needs into analytics solutions Own and grow dbt-based infrastructure to support operational workflows while utilizing development best practices Design and analyze experiments (A/B and multivariate) to measure effectiveness Improve attribution logic using best practices in analytics Build compelling visualizations and dashboards to support decision-making Collaborate with other data analysts on shared infrastructure and cross-functional deliverables Leverage Generative AI, Large Language Models (LLMs), and prompt engineering to build conversational analytics tools, automate insights, and enhance natural language data querying capabilities. Job Description: The Manager, Healthcare Analytics will design and build analytic solutions that will inform business decisions, drive operations, measure our performance and demonstrate our value as a company. They will partner with our Financial, Clinical, and Operational stakeholders to answer complex business and clinical questions that will empower Cityblock to support our members as effectively and efficiently as possible. Responsibilities: Establish credibility and trusted working partnerships with clinical, operational and research leaders to identify levers for broad system change, pull them, and measure and communicate the results. Design and execute evaluation and measurement plans that effectively assess the outcomes of Cityblock’s programs and initiatives. Leverage complex statistical and machine learning approaches to demonstrate clinical and financial outcomes associated with Cityblock projects and programs. Communicates results effectively through presentations, white papers, and peer reviewed publications. Implement and configure reporting technologies in the Google tech stack, as well as use DBT and SQL Perform complex descriptive analyses over production-scale data sets to drive business decisions. Critically interrogate and interpret data to spot patterns and explain data. Design reports to steer our value-driven business lines, including the development of traditional and non-traditional success metrics. Work with our clinical operations team to design and implement robust evaluation frameworks. Ensure data quality and validation and utilize a range of data cleansing techniques to correct both ad hoc and systemic issues. Drive automation capabilities into reporting solutions. Retain our analytics repository and other relevant documentation. Use AI-assisted workflows to accelerate the development, optimization, and management of dbt models and relevant semantic context, while designing and deploying conversational analytics tools to enable intuitive stakeholder self-service. Requirements: 5 years experience in advanced analytics, delivering actionable insights from data, database and big data analytics environments. 3 years of experience developing statistical and machine learning models to answer complex healthcare problems leveraging a wide range of techniques (generalized linear regression, negative binomial regression, k-means clustering, decision trees, etc.) We take into account an individual’s qualifications, skillset, and experience in determining final salary. Job Description: We are looking for an Engineer to add substantial value to cityblock’s technology platform used by our various engineering and product teams. We build and improve the core architecture of our user-facing systems, used by hundreds of care providers every day. You will join our Platform Engineering team whose goal is to make developing and deploying code at Cityblock more efficient, reliable, secure, and generally…pleasant! Our stakeholders are all of Cityblock’s engineers and we frequently pair or mob collaboratively with them to help understand their problems and create systemic solutions. Our existing applications generally use: Typescript, Node.js, GraphQL, TypeGraphQL, React, and PostgreSQL, and leverage GCP through Cloud Run, BigQuery, GCS and Cloud Functions. On the Platform Team, we also work heavily with tools like Terraform, Datadog, Github Actions, Tonic, and Apollo to automate management of these systems for other projects. We cross boundaries to partner with the data engineering team on cross-organizational platform management and also interact with python and DBT. We are looking for someone with experience working across the full stack and comfort with querying and analyzing data to drive decisions If you like building great developer experiences, are seeking out entrepreneurial opportunities, and are an amazing teammate, we want to hear from you! Responsibilities Design, build, and maintain cloud infrastructure on GCP using Terraform, with an emphasis on reusable modules, clear ownership boundaries, and safe change management. Own and evolve our CI/CD pipelines, developer environments, and deployment tooling to keep our software and data engineers productive. Execute infosec initiatives: vulnerability management, access controls, secrets management, audit logging, compliance evidence collection (HIPAA, SOC 2), and incident response. Build observability into our systems—metrics, logs, traces, alerting, and SLOs—so we can detect and resolve issues before they impact Members or care teams. Partner with software and data engineers to unblock them on infrastructure, database, networking, and deployment challenges, and provide paved-road patterns that make the secure path the easy path. Support our data platform, including infrastructure for dbt, data warehouses, orchestration, and pipelines that power clinical analytics and operations. Leverage AI-assisted development tools (Cursor, Claude Code) to accelerate Terraform development, automate toil, write runbooks and documentation, and tackle complex migrations—while maintaining rigorous standards for correctness, security, and maintainability. Drive cost visibility and optimization across our GCP footprint. Write clean, well-tested infrastructure code and automation that will stand the test of time. Participate in creating and maintaining strict compliance, data privacy, and security measures. Help recruit highly capable engineers to the team from diverse backgrounds. Mentor engineers and new team members—including sharing best practices for effective AI-assisted development and secure-by-default infrastructure patterns—while continuously upgrading your own skills. Collaborate with clinical teams, product managers, and designers when platform decisions affect end-user experience. Requirements for the Role You have 6+ years of experience in platform, infrastructure, DevOps, SRE, or a closely related engineering role. At least 2+ years of experience leading technical initiatives or members of an engineering team. You are invigorated by the complexities of building reliable, secure infrastructure for health tech. You have a process-oriented mindset and are comfortable writing and debating design documents, RFCs, and postmortems. Deep experience with Terraform (or a comparable IaC tool) in a production environment, including module design, state management, and safe rollout practices. Strong working knowledge of a major cloud provider (GCP preferred; AWS or Azure acceptable with willingness to learn GCP). Hands-on experience executing security and compliance work—IAM, secrets management, vulnerability remediation, audit preparation, or similar. Experience building and operating CI/CD pipelines and developer tooling. You have hands-on experience using AI coding assistants (such as Cursor, Claude Code, Copilot, or similar) as part of your daily workflow, and have developed good judgment about when to rely on them and when not to. You have a passion for doing mission-oriented work. You enjoy working with a diverse group of people with different expertise. We take into account an individual’s qualifications, skillset, and experience in determining final salary. This role is eligible for health insurance, life insurance, retirement benefits, participation in the company’s equity program, paid time off, including vacation and sick leave. The actual offer will be at the company’s sole discretion and determined by relevant business considerations, including the final candidate’s qualifications, years of experience, skillset, and geographic location. The expected salary range for this position is: $139,000.00 - $183,000.00 Annual Cityblock values diversity as a core tenet of the work we do and the populations we serve. Job Description: The Principal, Executive Operations & AI Enablement serves as the primary operational catalyst, executive governance lead, and AI enablement partner for the Executive Leadership Team (ELT) and Board operations. This is not a traditional administrative role; it is a high-impact, forward-looking position designed for an elite operator with exceptional executive presence, operational rigor, AI fluency, and judgment. The role combines three critical dimensions: AI-enabled executive workflow transformation and operational optimization End-to-end governance and executive operations management for the ELT and Board of Directors Strategic execution support for high-priority initiatives and executive priorities Acting as a trusted advisor and execution partner to senior leadership, this individual orchestrates executive and Board governance processes, drives operational excellence, and continuously identifies opportunities to leverage AI and automation to increase organizational efficiency and executive leverage. Success in this role requires obsessive attention to detail, exceptional organizational discipline, executive maturity, professionalism in high-stakes environments, and a relentless commitment to quality, precision, and follow-through. This individual must thrive in a fast-paced environment while seamlessly balancing strategic thinking with hands-on execution. Responsibilities: AI Prototyping & Workflow Transformation: Act as the internal benchmark for AI productivity. Partner 1:1 with ELT leaders to build custom AI assistants, engineer advanced prompts, automate legacy manual tasks, and introduce modern, scalable operating practices to maximize executive leverage. Executive Governance & Board Operations: Own the end-to-end strategy and precision execution of all ELT and Board of Directors meetings. Manage complex calendars, design high-impact agendas, track action items, and maintain absolute version control over critical governance assets. Materials & Communications QA: Serve as the final line of defense for executive outputs. Author, edit, and rigorously quality-assure Board decks, investor presentations, and strategic briefs for absolute polish, accuracy, and clarity. Strategic Initiatives & Special Projects: Partner directly with the CEO and C-suite to independently drive high-priority cross-functional initiatives under tight deadlines, proactively surfacing operational risks and clearing execution bottlenecks. Discretion & Trust: Handle highly confidential business matters with exceptional judgment, professionalism, and executive maturity. Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in Business, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field preferred. Familiarity with the operational, regulatory, and compliance considerations within value-based healthcare or high-growth technology environments is a strong plus. Overall Experience: 8+ years of progressive experience in Chief of Staff functions, strategic operations, management consulting, or high-level executive operations within fast-paced, high-growth environments. Executive Operations Expertise: Proven experience supporting executive leadership teams and Board governance processes, including executive meetings, Board administration, agenda management, executive communications, and confidential leadership operations. AI Technical Literacy: Advanced hands-on proficiency with modern generative AI tools and platforms (e.g., OpenAI, Claude, custom GPTs, Microsoft Copilot, AI-enabled workflow automation tools such as Zapier or Make, and productivity-enhancing AI applications). Tooling Expertise: Mastery of modern productivity and collaboration platforms including Google Workspace, Microsoft 365, Slack, Notion, Asana, ClickUp, Jira, Monday.com, or similar operational systems. Communication & Presence: Exceptional verbal and written communication skills with the professional presence required to comfortably influence senior executives, investors, and Board members. Execution Style: An unwavering ownership mindset with obsessive attention to detail, strong systems thinking, and a calm demeanor under high-stakes pressure. We take into account an individual’s qualifications, skillset, and experience in determining final salary. This role is eligible for health insurance, life insurance, retirement benefits, participation in the company’s equity program, paid time off, including vacation and sick leave. The actual offer will be at the company’s sole discretion and determined by relevant business considerations, including the final candidate’s qualifications, years of experience, skillset, and geographic location. The expected salary range for this position is: $125,000.00 - $165,000.00 Annual Cityblock values diversity as a core tenet of the work we do and the populations we serve.
